blocks.content: Welcome to ADK Foundations. Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK) is a modular, language-independent framework built to run autonomous AI agents in production. ADK structures agent loops, standardizes tool invocation formats, manages session memory boundaries, and facilitates integration with enterprise services.
Before writing code, it is critical to understand the four primary pillars of the ADK system architecture:

items.title: Agent Engine
items.content: The central core that coordinates semantic routing, planning, and task execution sequences using LLM backends.

items.title: Tool Registry
items.content: A structured library of functions that the agent can execute, configured with exact JSON Schemas.

items.title: Workspace Boundary
items.content: A localized sandboxed folder structure restricting the agent's file system read, write, and execute permissions.

items.title: Session Store
items.content: A stateful ledger tracking model requests, response steps, token consumption, and variables.

blocks.content: Verify that your developer machine meets the minimum system dependencies before installing the SDK CLI tool:

items: Python 3.10 or higher (required for Python tool bindings and scripts)
Node.js 18.x or higher (required for TypeScript tool decoration and local web tooling)
Go 1.20 or higher (optional, required if building high-throughput native extensions)
Docker, Podman, or Distrobox (strongly recommended for sandboxed workspace containment)

blocks.title: Pro-Tip: Runtime Verification
blocks.content: Ensure Python and Node are in your system PATH by running 'python --version' and 'node --version' in your active shell prior to bootstrapping.
Question 1: What is the primary role of the Session Store in the ADK architecture?

- A) It compiles custom Go binaries into OCI container images.
- B) It acts as a stateful ledger tracking conversational state, token counts, and execution traces.
- C) It is a firewall that blocks external HTTP traffic.
Feedback: Option B is correct. The Session Store tracks the step-by-step state of the agent run, including token usage and conversation memory.
Question 2: Which runtime engines are officially supported for writing custom ADK tool decorations?

- A) Python, TypeScript/Node.js, and Go.
- B) Only Ruby on Rails.
- C) Bash shell scripts exclusively.
Feedback: Option A is correct. ADK provides official language bindings and decorators for Python, Node.js/TypeScript, and Go.
